statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.name: minutes
statistics.description: The total number of minutes played.

statistics.name: assistTurnoverRatio
statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

statistics.name: offensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the offense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot.

article.description: Antonio Green had 20 points, eight rebounds and four 3-pointers on Friday night, and Texas-Rio Grande Valley won its home opener 78-56 over Texas A&M-Kingsville.
article.headline: Green's 20 lead Rio Grande Valley over A&M-Kingsville 78-56
article.linkText: Green's 20 lead Rio Grande Valley over A&M-Kingsville 78-56
article.story: EDINBURG, Texas -- Antonio Green had 20 points, eight rebounds and four 3-pointers on Friday night, and Texas-Rio Grande Valley won its home opener 78-56 over Texas A&M-Kingsville. The Vaqueros (1-2) opened the game with 9 straight points, and Green had 13 of his points in the first 14 minutes to help them build a 36-12 lead. Rio Grande Valley had its biggest advantage at 72-44 and led by no less than 17 in the second half. Dakota Slaughter added 16 points, Shaquille Hines had 12 points and 10 rebounds and J.J. Thompson scored 11 for Rio Grande Valley. Dj Weathers had 16 points and Jamar Goodwin scored 12 to lead the Division II Javelinas. Goodwin and Weathers had a pair of back-to-back 3-pointers in the second half, but A&M-Kingsville couldn't overcome 29.0 percent shooting for the game.
